title: 怪屋
summary:
After Sophia encourages her mother, Magda Leonidis, regarding her performance, she signals for the narrator to follow Inspector Taverner out of the room. The narrator asks Taverner about his identity and purpose, to which Taverner explains that his questioning is a tactic to assess the inhabitants and gather clues. He notes that while Margarita Leonidis's credibility is low, she may reveal much if not interrupted. Taverner's primary objective is to determine the motive behind the cyanide poisoning.

The pair encounters a large, clumsy man who leads them to the living room, where they meet Clementine Leonidis, the wife of Roger Leonidis. Clementine, a sharp and alert woman around fifty years old, acknowledges that the death was a murder. She admits to disliking her father-in-law's life goals and methods but expresses uncertainty regarding a potential affair between Brenda Leonidis and Lawrence Brown.

Roger Leonidis returns from a phone call in a state of intense anger, accusing an unnamed woman of being cold-blooded and suggesting she murdered his father. He expresses deep guilt over disappointing Aristide and declares a desire to strangle the killer. Clementine reprimands Roger for his outburst, noting that he is not capable of violence, after which Roger leaves the room in embarrassment. During questioning, Clementine provides her alibi, stating she was working at the Lembert Institute on Gour Road until six o'clock. She also notes that Roger was at the London Boxes House catering company before returning home on the day of the death.
